description: HDMI 1.4b to MIPI DSI dual-link bridge IC; converts up to 4K×2K/30fps HDMI stream (max 297 MHz, 7.2 Gbps) to dual-link MIPI DSI Tx at 1 Gbps/lane per lane, 80-pin VFBGA.

absolute_max.input_voltage: DSI IO: -0.3 to VDD12_MIPI+0.3V; Digital IO: -0.3 to VDDIO18+0.3V or VDDIO33+0.3V

key_performance.hdmi_input: HDMI 1.4b (up to 297 MHz TMDS clock, 7.2 Gbps stream)
key_performance.mipi_output: MIPI DSI Tx (dual-link DSI0 + DSI1), each 4 data lanes at 1 Gbps/lane
key_performance.dual_link_max: 4K×2K @ 30fps; max Hsync skew between DSI0 and DSI1 < 10 ByteClk
key_performance.color_formats: RGB888, RGB666, YCbCr444, YCbCr422 (16/24bpp); color conversion supported
key_performance.audio: Up to 4×I2S data lines (5.1/7.1), max 192 kHz @8CH; I2S and TDM pins multiplexed
key_performance.i2c_addresses: 0x0F (INT pulled low at RESETN) or 0x1F (INT pulled high at RESETN)

pins.name: RESETN
pins.description: System reset input, active low. Schmitt trigger. Powered by VDDIO18.
pins.requirements: Drive low ≥100µs to reset. Pull high for normal operation.

pins.name: REFCLK
pins.description: Reference clock input, 40–50 MHz. Schmitt trigger. Powered by VDDIO18.
pins.requirements: Must supply 40–50 MHz reference clock.

pins.name: TEST
pins.description: Internal test terminal. Normal digital I/O. Powered by VDDIO18.
pins.requirements: MANDATORY: Always must be fixed low externally — never leave floating.

pins.name: INT
pins.description: Dual-function: during RESETN asserted, input — detects pull polarity to select I2C slave address (pull-up → 0x1F, pull-down → 0x0F). After RESETN deasserted, becomes interrupt output (active high). Normal digital. Powered by VDDIO18.
pins.requirements: Pull up or pull down with 10kΩ external resistor. Consult Toshiba representative before board design to determine direction.

pins.name: CDSI0CP
pins.description: MIPI-DSI0 clock lane positive. MIPI-PHY output. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair to DSI panel.

pins.name: CDSI0CN
pins.description: MIPI-DSI0 clock lane negative. MIPI-PHY output. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair with CDSI0CP.

pins.name: CDSI0D0P
pins.description: MIPI-DSI0 data lane 0 positive. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair.

pins.name: CDSI0D0N
pins.description: MIPI-DSI0 data lane 0 negative. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair with CDSI0D0P.

pins.name: CDSI0D1P
pins.description: MIPI-DSI0 data lane 1 positive. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair.

pins.name: CDSI0D1N
pins.description: MIPI-DSI0 data lane 1 negative. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair with CDSI0D1P.

pins.name: CDSI0D2P
pins.description: MIPI-DSI0 data lane 2 positive. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair.

pins.name: CDSI0D2N
pins.description: MIPI-DSI0 data lane 2 negative. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair with CDSI0D2P.

pins.name: CDSI0D3P
pins.description: MIPI-DSI0 data lane 3 positive. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair.

pins.name: CDSI0D3N
pins.description: MIPI-DSI0 data lane 3 negative. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair with CDSI0D3P.

pins.name: CDSI1CP
pins.description: MIPI-DSI1 clock lane positive. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI0. DSI1 carries right half of HDMI video.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair.

pins.name: CDSI1CN
pins.description: MIPI-DSI1 clock lane negative. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI1.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair with CDSI1CP.

pins.name: CDSI1D0P
pins.description: MIPI-DSI1 data lane 0 positive. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI1.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair.

pins.name: CDSI1D0N
pins.description: MIPI-DSI1 data lane 0 negative. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI1.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair with CDSI1D0P.

pins.name: CDSI1D1P
pins.description: MIPI-DSI1 data lane 1 positive. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI1.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair.

pins.name: CDSI1D1N
pins.description: MIPI-DSI1 data lane 1 negative. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI1.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair with CDSI1D1P.

pins.name: CDSI1D2P
pins.description: MIPI-DSI1 data lane 2 positive. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI1.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair.

pins.name: CDSI1D2N
pins.description: MIPI-DSI1 data lane 2 negative. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI1.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair with CDSI1D2P.

pins.name: CDSI1D3P
pins.description: MIPI-DSI1 data lane 3 positive. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI1.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair.

pins.name: CDSI1D3N
pins.description: MIPI-DSI1 data lane 3 negative. MIPI-PHY. Powered by VDD12_MIPI1.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω differential pair with CDSI1D3P.

pins.name: HDMICP
pins.description: HDMI clock channel positive. HDMI-PHY. Powered by VDD33_HDMI.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω TMDS differential pair from HDMI connector.

pins.name: HDMICN
pins.description: HDMI clock channel negative. HDMI-PHY. Powered by VDD33_HDMI.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω TMDS differential pair with HDMICP.

pins.name: HDMID0P
pins.description: HDMI data channel 0 positive. HDMI-PHY. Powered by VDD33_HDMI.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω TMDS differential pair.

pins.name: HDMID0N
pins.description: HDMI data channel 0 negative. HDMI-PHY. Powered by VDD33_HDMI.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω TMDS differential pair with HDMID0P.

pins.name: HDMID1P
pins.description: HDMI data channel 1 positive. HDMI-PHY. Powered by VDD33_HDMI.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω TMDS differential pair.

pins.name: HDMID1N
pins.description: HDMI data channel 1 negative. HDMI-PHY. Powered by VDD33_HDMI.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω TMDS differential pair with HDMID1P.

pins.name: HDMID2P
pins.description: HDMI data channel 2 positive. HDMI-PHY. Powered by VDD33_HDMI.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω TMDS differential pair.

pins.name: HDMID2N
pins.description: HDMI data channel 2 negative. HDMI-PHY. Powered by VDD33_HDMI.
pins.requirements: Route as 100Ω TMDS differential pair with HDMID2P.

pins.name: REXT
pins.description: External reference resistor terminal for HDMI-RX automatic termination resistance adjustment. Powered by VDD33_HDMI.
pins.requirements: MANDATORY: Connect 2kΩ±1% between VDD33_HDMI and this pin. Required per Fig 5.2 p.16.

pins.name: DDC_SCL
pins.description: DDC I2C slave clock. Schmitt trigger, 5V tolerant, open drain. Powered by VDDIO33.
pins.requirements: Pull up to +5V via 1kΩ±5%. Add 100kΩ pull-down to GND to hold low when 5V is off. See Fig 5.1 p.15.

pins.name: DDC_SDA
pins.description: DDC I2C slave data. Schmitt trigger, 5V tolerant, open drain. Powered by VDDIO33.
pins.requirements: Pull up to +5V via 4.7kΩ±5%. Add 100kΩ pull-down to GND to hold low when 5V is off. See Fig 5.1 p.15.

pins.name: CEC
pins.description: HDMI CEC signal. Open drain. Powered by VDDIO33. Leave NC if not used.

pins.name: HPDI
pins.description: Hot Plug Detect input, 5V tolerant. Connected to HDMI pin 18 (+5V). Powered by VDDIO33.
pins.requirements: Pull up to +5V via 100kΩ±5% per Fig 5.1 p.15.

pins.name: HPDO
pins.description: Hot Plug Detect output, active low. Normal digital output. Powered by VDDIO33.

pins.name: A_SCK
pins.description: I2S / TDM bit clock signal output. Powered by VDDIO18.

pins.name: A_WFS
pins.description: I2S word clock / TDM frame sync output. Powered by VDDIO18.

pins.name: A_SD3
pins.description: I2S audio data signal bit 3 output. Powered by VDDIO18.

pins.name: A_SD2
pins.description: I2S audio data signal bit 2 output. Powered by VDDIO18.

pins.name: A_SD1
pins.description: I2S audio data signal bit 1 output. Powered by VDDIO18.

pins.name: A_SD0
pins.description: I2S audio data bit 0 / TDM data output. Powered by VDDIO18.

pins.name: A_OSCK
pins.description: Audio Over Sampling Clock output (256fs). Powered by VDDIO18.

pins.name: IR
pins.description: InfraRed signal input supporting NEC protocol. Normal digital input. Powered by VDDIO18.
pins.requirements: Fix low externally if not used.

pins.name: I2C_SCL
pins.description: I2C slave clock. Open drain. Supports 100 kHz / 400 kHz / 2 MHz. Powered by VDDIO18.
pins.requirements: External pull-up resistor required.

pins.name: I2C_SDA
pins.description: I2C slave data. Open drain. Powered by VDDIO18.
pins.requirements: External pull-up resistor required.

pins.name: BIASDA
pins.description: Audio PLL BIAS signal output. Powered by VDDIO33.
pins.requirements: Connect to AVSS through 0.1μF when Audio PLL not used. See Fig 5.3 p.16.

pins.name: DAOUT
pins.description: Audio PLL Clock Reference output. PLL clock — external LPF required. Powered by VDDIO33.
pins.requirements: Connect LPF per Fig 5.3 p.16: 5pF to GND, 1.0kΩ, 2SC2712Y transistor, 1000pF to GND. Leave open if not used.

pins.name: PCKIN
pins.description: Audio PLL Reference Input clock. PLL input. Powered by VDDIO33.
pins.requirements: Connect to AVSS through 0.1μF when Audio PLL not used. See Fig 5.3 p.16.

pins.name: PFIL
pins.description: Audio PLL Low Pass Filter signal for phase comparator. Powered by VDDIO33.
pins.requirements: Connect 0.1μF and 1000pF to AVSS per Fig 5.3 p.16 when not used.

pins.name: VDDC11
pins.description: 1.1V internal core power supply (ball C10).
pins.requirements: Decouple with >1μF and >0.1μF to VSS. Separate from regulator VSS via ferrite bead.

pins.name: VDDC11
pins.description: 1.1V internal core power supply (ball K6).
pins.requirements: Decouple with >1μF and >0.1μF to VSS. Separate from regulator VSS via ferrite bead.

pins.name: VDDIO18
pins.description: 1.8V IO supply for system, audio, IR, I2C interfaces. Can also operate at 3.3V.
pins.requirements: Decouple with >10μF, >1μF, >0.1μF to VSS. Separate via ferrite bead.

pins.name: VDDIO33
pins.description: 3.3V IO supply for DDC, CEC, HPD, Audio PLL interfaces.
pins.requirements: Decouple with >1μF and >0.1μF to VSS. MUST be isolated from VDD33_HDMI — ESD diode reverse current at power-off damages VDDIO33.

pins.name: VDD33_HDMI
pins.description: HDMI PHY 3.3V power supply (ball B1).
pins.requirements: MANDATORY: Isolate from VDDIO33 and all other 3.3V supplies — ESD diode reverse current at power-off will damage other supplies. Decouple with >10μF×3, >1μF, >0.1μF. See Fig 5.4 p.17.

pins.name: VDD33_HDMI
pins.description: HDMI PHY 3.3V power supply (ball G1).
pins.requirements: MANDATORY: Same isolation requirement as ball B1. See Fig 5.4 p.17.

pins.name: VDD11_HDMI
pins.description: HDMI PHY 1.1V power supply (ball B2).
pins.requirements: Decouple with >10μF×2, >1μF, >0.1μF. Separate via ferrite bead.

pins.name: VDD11_HDMI
pins.description: HDMI PHY 1.1V power supply (ball G2).
pins.requirements: Decouple with >10μF×2, >1μF, >0.1μF. Separate via ferrite bead.

pins.name: VDD12_MIPI0
pins.description: MIPI DSI 1.2V supply for link 0 (DSI0 / CDSI TX0).
pins.requirements: Decouple with >10μF×2, >1μF, >0.1μF. Separate via ferrite bead.

pins.name: VDD12_MIPI1
pins.description: MIPI DSI 1.2V supply for link 1 (DSI1 / CDSI TX1).
pins.requirements: Decouple with >10μF×2, >1μF, >0.1μF. Separate via ferrite bead.

pins.name: VSS
pins.description: Ground (ball A10).
pins.requirements: Connect to ground plane. Separate at AC from regulator GND via ferrite bead for EMI.

required_passives.reason: Selects I2C slave address during RESETN assertion. Consult Toshiba before board design.

required_passives.value: FB (ferrite bead) on each supply rail
required_passives.placement: Between regulator output and each IC power rail
required_passives.reason: REQUIRED: All TC358870 VSS must be separated at AC level from regulator VSS via ferrite bead to attenuate EMI. See Fig 5.4 p.17.

application_circuits.name: Typical HDMI to Dual DSI system (Fig 1.1, p.7)
application_circuits.description: HDMI source → HDMICP/N + HDMID0-2P/N (TMDS differential) → internal TMDS Rx PHY → YUV/RGB FiFo → dual-link DSI Tx. DSI0 path: DSITX0 engine → CDSI0CP/N (clock) + CDSI0D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) → DSI panel left half. DSI1 path: DSITX1 engine → CDSI1CP/N (clock) + CDSI1D0-3P/N (4 data lanes) → DSI panel right half. DDC: HDMI connector → DDC_SDA/SCL with 5V pull-ups and GND pull-downs (Fig 5.1) for EDID access. HPD: HPDI from HDMI +5V pin, HPDO outputs hot plug detect. Host → I2C_SCL/SDA to configure all registers. INT output to host for interrupt signalling.

application_circuits.name: DDC Interface Connection (Fig 5.1, p.15)
application_circuits.description: HDMI connector DDC_SCL → 1kΩ pull-up to +5V and 100kΩ pull-down to GND → DDC_SCL (A3). DDC_SDA → 4.7kΩ pull-up to +5V and 100kΩ pull-down to GND → DDC_SDA (B3). HPDI (A4) → 100kΩ to +5V. HPDO (B4) drives hot plug detect back to HDMI connector.

application_circuits.name: Audio Clock External LPF (Fig 5.3, p.16)
application_circuits.description: VDD33_HDMI → 1.5kΩ bias → BIASDA (J1) [0.1μF to GND]. DAOUT (J2) → 5pF to GND → 2SC2712Y NPN transistor base → collector via 1.0kΩ → PCKIN (K1) [0.1μF to GND]; transistor emitter via 1.0kΩ to GND. PFIL (K2) → 0.1μF + 1000pF to GND.

application_circuits.name: Power supply with VDD33_HDMI isolation (Fig 5.4, p.17)
application_circuits.description: 5V → 3.3V reg → external switch on TMDS signal lines → VDD33_HDMI (>10μF×3 + >1μF + >0.1μF to VSS). Separate: 1.15V reg → FB → VDD11_HDMI; 1.15V reg → FB → VDDC11; 3.3V reg → FB → VDDIO33; 1.8V reg → FB → VDDIO18; 1.2V reg → FB → VDD12_MIPI0/VDD12_MIPI1. All VSS pins separated from regulator GND via ferrite bead.

common_mistakes: TEST pin (G5) ALWAYS must be fixed low externally — never leave floating or pull high
IR pin (G6): Fix low externally if not used — do not leave floating
INT pin (J3) is dual-function: during RESETN it is a bootstrap INPUT for I2C address; after RESETN it automatically becomes the INT OUTPUT. The 10kΩ pull resistor serves both functions.

VDD33_HDMI MUST be isolated from VDDIO33 and all other 3.3V supplies. ESD diode on TMDS inputs causes reverse current into VDD33_HDMI at power-off which also damages VDDIO33. Use external switch (Fig 5.4) or reverse-current-protection regulator (Fig 5.5).
BIASDA, PCKIN, PFIL MUST be connected to AVSS through 0.1μF when Audio PLL is not used
REXT (A1) MUST have 2kΩ±1% to VDD33_HDMI — mandatory for HDMI-RX termination calibration
DDC_SDA and DDC_SCL must have pull-down resistors to hold lines low when +5V power to HDMI connector is absent
All TC358870 VSS balls MUST be separated at AC level from regulator VSS using ferrite beads to attenuate EMI
Max Hsync skew between DSI0 and DSI1 must be less than 10 ByteClk cycles in dual-link mode
No-ball positions (C3–C8, D3, D8, E3, E8, F3, F8, G3, G8, H3–H8) are physically absent — do not attempt connections at these grid positions
notes: 80-ball P-VFBGA80 BGA, 7×7mm, 0.65mm pitch, 1.0mm height. Requires multi-layer PCB with BGA via fanout. I2C slave addresses: 0x0F (INT pulled down at RESETN) or 0x1F (INT pulled up at RESETN). HDCP 1.4 decryption supported (optional). EDID: embedded 1K-byte SRAM (CEA-861-D). DSI0 carries left half of HDMI video, DSI1 carries right half — both clocks and data widths can differ. REFCLK must be 40–50 MHz. Reverse current protection on VDD33_HDMI is the most critical board-level concern.
